# HED (Hierarchical Event Descriptors)

> HED is a standardized, machine-readable vocabulary for describing what happened during an
> experiment, linking brain, body, behavior, and experience through metadata. It is a standard
> with a versioned schema, Python and JavaScript validators, an online web service, and a Model
> Context Protocol (MCP) server. Used in the Brain Imaging Data Structure (BIDS); endorsed by
> INCF, BIDS, NWB, NEMAR, and OpenNeuro.

## Start here
- [What HED is](https://www.hedtags.org): the introduction and the roles it serves (experimenter, annotator, analyst, tool developer, schema builder)
- [HED Resources](https://www.hed-resources.org): tutorials, guides, and worked examples
- [Schema browser](https://www.hedtags.org): explore the HED vocabulary
- [Online tools](https://hedtools.org/hed): validate, assemble, and remodel in the browser (latest features: https://hedtools.org/hed_dev)

## The standard
- [Specification](https://github.com/hed-standard/hed-specification): the HED spec, versioned and citable
- [Schemas](https://github.com/hed-standard/hed-schemas): the vocabulary in XML, MediaWiki, and TSV, versioned (for example 8.3.0)
- [HED in BIDS](https://bids.neuroimaging.io): how HED annotates events in BIDS datasets

## Validate and build (tools)
- [Python tools](https://github.com/hed-standard/hed-python): `pip install hedtools`, for validation and remodeling
- [JavaScript validator](https://github.com/hed-standard/hed-javascript): `npm install hed-validator`
- [Web service](https://hedtools.org/hed): browser tools plus a JSON web service (docs: https://hed-web.readthedocs.io)
- [MCP server](https://github.com/hed-standard/hed-mcp): validate HED strings, BIDS TSV files, and JSON sidecars from any MCP client

## Notes for agents
- HED is machine-checkable by design. Generate an annotation, then validate it, do not guess. Use Python, JavaScript, the web service, or the MCP server.
- The schema is versioned; target a specific version and check compatibility before validating.
